examples: the bombs are dropping on enemy targets

meanings.definition: fall or descend to a lower place or level

examples: drop him from the Republican ticket

meanings.definition: utter with seeming casualness; ; drop names"

examples: drop off the passengers at the hotel

meanings.definition: cause to fall by or as if by delivering a blow

examples: The Giants dropped 11 of their first 13
the ornaments dangled from the tree
The light dropped from the ceiling
They dropped her after she had a child out of wedlock

meanings.definition: let or cause to fall in drops

examples: he shed his image as a pushy boss

meanings.definition: take (a drug, especially LSD), by mouth
examples: She dropped acid when she was a teenager

meanings.definition: omit (a letter or syllable) in speaking or writing
examples: New Englanders drop their post-vocalic r's
How could I miss that typo?
The workers on the conveyor belt miss one out of ten

meanings.definition: change from one level to another
fall or sink into a state of exhaustion or death

examples: The discussion devolved into a shouting match
The cow dropped her calf this morning

meanings.definition: coming down freely under the influence of gravity
examples: the eerie whistle of dropping bombs
